arts.ttl: Rodgers keen for Celtic to use home atmosphere and ‘hurt’ Bayern Munich
arts.sum: Scottish side welcome ‘football royalty’ on Wednesday Hosts boosted by Daizen Maeda return after ban appeal Brendan Rodgers wants Bayern Munich to experience “hurt” at Celtic Park after taking pride in his team’s European improvement this season. Bayern’s visit to Glasgow marks the first time since 2013 that Celtic will play knockout Champions League football, with Rodgers contemplating his most significant scalp yet. Celtic are the heavy underdogs to see off the German giants over two legs but Rodgers has challenged his players to keep the tie competitive before they head to the Allianz Arena next week. Slovan Bratislava, RB Leipzig and Young Boys have already been defeated at Celtic Park this season, with the hosts unbeaten in their last half-dozen home European games. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Celtic manager provides update on Carter-Vickers’ chances of facing Bayern Munich
arts.sum: Cameron Carter-Vickers is set to feature for Celtic in their upcoming match against Bayern Munich.

arts.ttl: Man City & Celtic face Real & Bayern - but who else is in play-offs?
arts.sum: The two-legged Champions League knockout phase play-offs get under way this week with Manchester City and Celtic among the 16 teams looking to reach the last 16.

arts.ttl: Harry Kane turns silver into gold as Celtic boss Brendan Rodgers assesses Bayern hitman's journey to stardom
arts.sum: The Hoops gaffer poured major praise on the England captain who's battled his way to the very top of the game

arts.ttl: What we learned from Celtic's final training session before Bayern Munich Champions League tie
arts.sum: Had he not suffered with that issue it’s likely he’d have played in the Cup but it could be a blessing in disguise that Viljami Sinisalo got 90 minutes with Schmeichel now back and ready for Bayern. It’s almost go time at Celtic Park ahead of tomorrow evening’s Champions League tie vs Bayern Munich.

arts.ttl: Rodgers applauds Celtic’s financial strength ahead of crucial UCL clash with Bayern
arts.sum: Brendan Rodgers feels that he’s been very well backed by the Celtic board.

arts.ttl: Brendan Rodgers admits Celtic were handed 'big surprise' for Bayern Munich as boss expected different outcome
arts.sum: Rodgers did not expect the club to win the appeal and played Maeda in the three most recent clashes with Motherwell, Dundee and then Raith Rovers respectively. Daizen Maeda Celtic appeal ‘nice surprise’ to Rodgers

arts.ttl: Daizen Maeda Celtic surprise catches Brendan Rodgers out as triple shift sparks 'I've run the legs off him' realisation
arts.sum: The Celtic boss has his top scorer available after the club won an appeal against his two-match suspension

arts.ttl: ‘I truly back us against anybody’ – Trusty confident Celtic can stun Bayern Munich
arts.sum: Auston Trusty 'truly believes' Celtic can beat Bayern Munich in the UEFA Champions League this Wednesday.

arts.ttl: Midweek Warm-up: Is Bayern Munich about to raid Crystal Palace of another young winger?; Throwing it back to Van Halen; Champions League predictions; Reviewing and MORE!
arts.sum: Crystal Palace starlet Jesse Derry Boy is a target for a number of top Euro clubs, including Bayern Munich, Juventus, PSG and Atletico Madrid. Crystal Palace youngster Jesse Derry is set to leave the club after rejecting a professional contract.
